publications.editedComment: Six rooms, each watched by a different eye. Refresh initializes navigation and, when needed, the server store. A guest on Dashboard receives Welcome, guest and a Log In path; a signed-in user gets the dashboard and a guarded Log Out that redirects to `/login`. Other tabs open avatar selection, friends, achievements, themes, or chats, while an unknown tab is named instead of silently swallowed. Choosing or closing the avatar picker returns to Dashboard. Loading and manager errors remain visible. No eye sees everything, but every account state has a room and a way back.

Here’s the tighter version: Dashboard distinguishes guests from signed-in users. Guests get a clear Log In route; members get Log Out, and the button cannot fire twice while logout runs. Refresh initializes navigation and reloads remote server data when forced. The selected navigation tab routes to dashboard, avatar picker, friend gallery, achievements, themes, or chats; invalid tabs produce a visible warning. Selecting an avatar or closing its picker returns to Dashboard. Loading and error states stay on the page instead of becoming mystery silence. Account hub, polished: clear verbs, clear states, clean exit.

The image is treated as something that can change without forgetting its origin. Gallery and Deselect return to the larger collection; Set avatar appears for signed-in users; Remix sends a new prompt toward the generator. Metadata edits compare a trimmed payload with the original before Save can run, while checkpoint, sampler, seed, CFG, visibility, maturity, and prompt text remain visible. Collection membership can be searched and toggled, and a new collection can be created in place. Deletion belongs only to an administrator or owner and requires a second Confirm click. Tomorrow is not a replacement for today; the source, edits, and decision gates remain beside one another.

Selected image. Goal: edit deliberately. The panel shows generation source, checkpoint, sampler, seed, and server beside an image-card preview. Quick Edit writes designer, prompts, checkpoint, sampler, steps, seed, CFG, public, and mature fields; Save activates only when the normalized payload differs from the stored image. Collection search and toggles update membership, FacetPicker handles art facets, and Remix passes the written instruction back to the generator. Set avatar requires login. Delete requires ownership or admin access plus an armed second click. When no image is selected, Open Gallery is the only next instruction. Prompt, state, action—kept in that order.

Seven drawers and one noisy workbench. Generate opens art-maker; Gallery opens art-gallery; Checkpoints shows checkpoint-gallery; Styler pairs art-styler with image-upload; Stylist, Art Test, and Coloring each receive their own manager. ArtJob places the job manager beside feedback, and clicking a finished data-image opens a contained preview dialog. Loading names images, collections, checkpoints, and the pixel goblin infrastructure; errors keep a Retry button. Initialization loads servers, art records, and collections together, then selects Euler a when no sampler exists. The old Upload tab is filed under Styler. Nothing is thrown out merely because the labels changed.

Route the request by tab. `generate` renders art-maker; `gallery` renders art-gallery; `checkpoints` renders checkpoint-gallery; `styler` combines art-styler and image-upload; `stylist`, `art-test`, and `coloring` load their dedicated tools; `artjob` pairs job management with feedback. A legacy `upload` selection resolves to Styler. `loadManagerData` initializes remote servers, art data without eager image hydration, and collections in parallel, then supplies Euler a when no sampler is selected. Errors expose Retry. Finished ArtJob data-image links are intercepted into a modal preview and cleared on close. One manager, explicit branches, no guessed destination.

Name and status ride at the top; description follows only when requested. The card can expose Select, Edit, and Test health, each controlled by its own flag. Type, Access, Auth, and Owner sit in separate boxes, while Base, Endpoint, and Health paths remain in the metadata section. A1111 and COMFY servers may be chosen for Art; OPENAI, ANTHROPIC, and CUSTOM servers may be chosen for Text. Official, Public, and Private ownership labels come from the record. Selected styling also recognizes the current server and active art or text servers. Plain hardware facts, laid out before anyone asks the dust to pretend.

Read the plate before striking the metal. The header binds the server’s icon, label, and last status; ONLINE, OFFLINE, and DEGRADED choose distinct badges. Action flags control Select, Edit, and Test health. The body stamps Type, Access, Auth, and Owner, then can reveal Base URL, endpoint path, and health path. A1111 or COMFY unlocks Use for Art. OPENAI, ANTHROPIC, or CUSTOM unlocks Use for Text. Selection follows the explicit prop, current server, active art server, or active text server. Edit opens the server form; Test calls the health check by id. Every hammer blow lands on a declared field.

The closed box shows New collection; opening it reveals one name field and, when requested, Public and Mature flags. The label is trimmed before creation, and an empty name receives the honest warning that folders are not psychic. While saving, Cancel and Create are disabled. `collectionStore.createCollection` receives the clean label and flags. Success emits `created`, optionally selects the new collection, resets the form, and closes it when `closeOnCreate` is enabled. Selection sets both the current collection and selected id list before emitting `selected`. A small cabinet, yes—but every new compartment is labeled, announced, and placed where it can be found again.

Collection copy needs a title before it needs sparkle. New collection opens the form; Cancel closes and resets it unless a save is active. The label is trimmed, Create stays disabled for blank text, and an empty submission produces a specific naming error. Optional Public and Mature toggles are shown only when `showFlags` is enabled. The store creates the collection from label and flags, then the component emits `created`. With the default `autoSelect`, it also sets the current collection, replaces the selected id list, and emits `selected`; default `closeOnCreate` folds the form afterward. Clean product organization starts with one dependable folder.

First, the conditions: a trimmed prompt, the required server, and—when the active server is A1111—a selected checkpoint. Until those exist, the button is disabled and the status line names what is missing. The prompt preview is optional and capped at 260 characters. `syncPromptToArtForm` copies prompt, title, and overrides into the store before generation. Failure sets a local error, records it in errorStore when appropriate, and emits `failed`. Success stores the returned ArtImage, derives an image path from path or base64 data, emits an update containing prompt, path, id, and image, then emits `generated`. A first draft that records every outcome can become a second.

Input: prompt, purpose, model title, image role, icon, preview preference, and generation overrides. Readiness requires a nonempty prompt, a server unless the engine is OpenAI or Kontext, and a checkpoint when the active server is A1111. The status label reports the missing prerequisite or active generation. On Generate, the component synchronizes prompt, title, and overrides into the art form, calls `generateCurrentArt`, and emits `failed` on error. On success it resolves imagePath or base64 imageData, displays the returned image-card, emits an update with prompt, path, ArtImage id, and object, then emits `generated`. Output is explicit; no silent branch.
